body.title: [sprint] Running the test suite writes junk proof records into the canonical memory store
body.intention: I intend that running the test suite leaves the canonical memory store byte-identical, so a dirty tree after pytest always means real work and never test debris.
body.description:
Observed 2026-07-09: `pytest tests/test_proof_emitter.py tests/test_proof_close_gate.py` wrote 5 untracked records into devlab/runtime/memory/proofs/ — cc.0.add-sums.T-proof-emitter-harness.*, cc.0.add-sums.T-x.*, cc.0.add-sums.T-ticket-close-requires-proof.*, cc.0.add-sums-two-numbers.*, cc.0.flag-removed.*. They are harness fixtures escaping into the real store.

Two harms. (1) proof_emitter refuses a dirty tree, so a test run silently blocks the next proof until someone notices and deletes files by hand — I hit exactly that today. (2) A store that accumulates fake proofs is a store whose proof corpus cannot be trusted, and the proof corpus is what earns the gates' eventual removal.

**Affected files:** tests/test_proof_emitter.py, tests/test_proof_close_gate.py, devlab/claudecode/proof_emitter.py (root resolution), possibly unseen_university/memory_root.py.
**Design rules:** hermetic proofs (never touch live state); canonical memory home devlab/runtime/memory/; UU_MEMORY_ROOT already exists as the redirect used by tests/test_ticket_store.py — the pattern is there, these tests just do not use it.
**Scope boundary:** IN — redirect these tests' emissions to tmp_path via UU_MEMORY_ROOT (or the equivalent monkeypatched root), and add a guard that the store is unchanged after the suite runs. OUT — reworking proof_emitter's dirty-tree check (it is correct); auditing historical proofs for fakes.
**Test plan:** run the two suites with the real store under git; assert `git status --porcelain devlab/runtime/memory/proofs/` is empty afterwards. Red form: at HEAD it is non-empty (5 files).
**Completion criteria:** after a full `pytest tests/`, `git status --porcelain devlab/runtime/memory/` is empty; no test writes outside tmp_path.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.completion_criteria: git status --porcelain devlab/runtime/memory/ is empty after a full test run; the two proof suites emit only under tmp_path.
